content:
Large Language Models (LLMs) generate text based on patterns learned during training.

They do not have real-time access to databases unless integrated with external systems.

LLMs can hallucinate when they lack sufficient context.

Providing structured context improves response accuracy.

Gemini is

red context improves response accuracy.

Gemini is an example of a large language model that can be used in RAG systems.

RAG stands for Retrieval Augmented Generation.

RAG improves large language models by providing external knowledge during inference.

The pipeline includes:
- document ingestion
- chunking
- embeddings
- storage
- retrieval
- prompt augmentation
- generation

RAG is useful when models do not have ac

neration

RAG is useful when models do not have access to private or recent data.

RAG systems reduce hallucinations by grounding responses in retrieved context.

A vector database stores embeddings.

Embeddings are numerical representations of text.

Vector databases support similarity search using cosine similarity or dot product.

Examples include FAISS, Pinecone, Weaviate, and Chroma.

Vector search helps retrieve semantically similar documents even when
retrieve semantically similar documents even when keywords differ.
